In this video, we will also learn that there is a hidden tool in Chrome called Lighthouse.

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTM
chunks.text: SEO stands for Search Engine Optimization.  If you see any website, it has SEO in it.  Let's take the example of Amazon.in.  I opened Amazon.in.  So Amazon.in wants this website to be loaded as soon as possible.

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTM
chunks.text: So that their products can come in search.  Google is a search engine.  Google's responsibility is to show the best pages to the users.  So Google wants to show only those pages that are fast.  They present the information very well.

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTM
chunks.text: And along with that, there are some things in them that the user has a good experience.  For example, let's say there are two pages.  One page has told about dog food.  The other page has also told about dog food exactly the same thing.  But the page 2 of these two is loaded very fast.

But if you are competing with a page that has presented almost similar quality content.  Then your core web vitals will be seen.  And if your core web vitals are good, then you will be given preference.

chunks.text: The next metric we are going to talk about.  That is LCP.  And LCP stands for Largest Contextual Paint.  This means that if the user is viewing a page.  Then the biggest element on it.

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTM
chunks.text: For example, the biggest part in this is this image.  How long is it loading?  Means the whole page will be loaded.  But the biggest looking element.  Which is the most important.

chunks.text: How long is it loading?  If we assume web.dev and Google.  Then a good LCP score should be 2.5 seconds.  Means the biggest element should be loaded in 2.5 seconds.  After this comes the third metric.

chunks.title: SEO and Core Web Vitals in HTM
chunks.text: Which is FID.  Which stands for First Input Delay.  First Input Delay means.  If any user has clicked on any input element.  Like on a button or any type of input.
